app.title: LLM Platform
chat.welcomeDesc: Start a conversation. Your messages are processed through enterprise DLP to protect sensitive data.
chat.placeholder: Type a message... (Enter to send, Shift+Enter for new line)
chat.attachHint: Send a message first to start a conversation
chat.thinking.autoDesc: Auto — the model/provider decides. DeepSeek V4 Flash defaults off, V4 Pro defaults on; GLM 5.x defaults on; OpenAI depends on the model.
chat.thinking.deepseekNote: DeepSeek only supports high/max — low maps to high, none disables thinking. OpenAI and GLM have meaningful differences between levels. Reasoning content is visible in the response.
chat.thinking.title: Thinking
keys.title: API Key Management
keys.desc: Manage your virtual API keys for programmatic access
keys.emptyHint: Generate your first key to get started
keys.warning: This key will only be shown once. Copy it now and store it securely.
keys.deleteWarningDesc: This key is currently active. Deleting it will permanently remove it from both LiteLLM and the local database. The key will stop working immediately for anyone using it.
providers.title: API Providers
providers.desc: Manage LLM provider models and API keys
providers.noModelsHint: Add your first LLM provider model to get started
providers.platformSettingsDesc: Configure platform-wide settings. Changes take effect immediately — no restart required.
providers.litellmUrlHint: Shown to users after they generate an API key
providers.litellmUrlDesc: This is the base URL users configure in their SDKs (e.g., OpenAI base_url, Anthropic base_url). LiteLLM proxy runs on port 4000 — use DNS/nginx to map this domain to the LiteLLM container.
providers.apiKeyHint: The API key from the LLM provider. Stored securely and never shown again.
providers.contextHint: LiteLLM defaults to 128K if not set. For DeepSeek 1M context, enter 1000.
providers.hideFromChatDesc: When enabled, this model won't appear in the chat picker but can still be authorized via API keys.
providers.deleteDesc: This model will no longer be available for chat or API use. This action cannot be undone.
providers.keyPlaceholder: Leave blank to keep current key
providers.keyHint: Current key is hidden for security. Enter a new key to replace it.
providers.customModelHint: Enter the full LiteLLM model path (provider/model-name). See
providers.modelIdHint: Provider model name. LiteLLM will call this as
ldap.title: LDAP Configuration
ldap.desc: Manage Windows AD / LDAP authentication sources
ldap.noSourcesHint: Add an AD / LDAP source to enable domain authentication
ldap.authNameHint: Display name shown on the login page
ldap.bindDnHint: A read-only service account DN. Do NOT use a domain admin account.
ldap.passwordHint: ⚠ Password is stored in Keycloak's database in reversible form. Use a low-privilege read-only account.
ldap.usersDnHint: The base DN where Keycloak searches for user accounts. Use the root (DC=...) for the widest coverage, or narrow to a specific OU.
ldap.advancedHint: These are pre-filled with Windows AD defaults (sAMAccountName / objectGUID). Only change if your directory uses different attribute names.
ldap.deleteDesc: This deletes the LDAP provider from Keycloak. Domain users will no longer be able to log in via this source.
ldap.syncTriggered: Sync triggered for "{name}". Check Keycloak admin for progress.
ldap.allFieldsRequired: All fields except the advanced attribute names are required.
stats.title: Token Usage Statistics
stats.desc: Aggregated token consumption by user and API key
stats.dateError: Start date must be on or before end date.
stats.noData: No usage data found for this period.
usage.title: My Usage
usage.onlyOwn: You can only view your own usage.
notFound.title: Page not found
settings.title: Settings
